turns.utterance: Can you pull up the July gardening draft? I need to get this out before the weekend.
Got it. 'Why Most Beginner Gardens Die in July', a draft with six sections: intro, the-july-problem, watering-mistakes, soil-heat, what-survives, closing. Ready to work on it.
The middle sections are repeating the same point about heat three times. Can you consolidate them into something that flows?

source.post: Why Most Beginner Gardens Die in July

turns.utterance: Restructured the middle. Merged watering-mistakes and soil-heat into one section called 'the heat trap' that treats both as a single feedback loop: less water means hotter soil means faster water loss. The repetition is gone. You still have five sections total.
The closing feels rushed. Can you give it a bit more room to breathe?

source.post: Why Most Beginner Gardens Die in July

turns.utterance: Expanded the closing. It now ends on the 'August reset' idea: most beginners who lose their summer plants do a second planting and it works because the worst heat is behind them. Gives the piece a forward-looking note instead of just cataloguing what went wrong.
Good. Does it still sound like me or did the rewrite drift the voice?
